2604.20805.title: Relative Principals, Pluralistic Alignment, and the Structural Value Alignment Problem
2604.20805.paper_summary: The value alignment problem for artificial intelligence (AI) is often framed as a purely technical or normative challenge, sometimes focused on hypothetical future systems. I argue that the problem is better understood as a structural question about governance: not whether an AI system is aligned in the abstract, but whether it is aligned enough, for whom, and at what cost. Drawing on the principal-agent framework from economics, this paper reconceptualises misalignment as arising along three interacting axes: objectives, information, and principals. The three-axis framework provides a systematic way of diagnosing why misalignment arises in real-world systems and clarifies that alignment cannot be treated as a single technical property of models but an outcome shaped by how objectives are specified, how information is distributed, and whose interests count in practice. The core contribution of this paper is to show that the three-axis decomposition implies that alignment is fundamentally a problem of governance rather than engineering alone. From this perspective, alignment is inherently pluralistic and context-dependent, and resolving misalignment involves trade-offs among competing values. Because misalignment can occur along each axis -- and affect stakeholders differently -- the structural description shows that alignment cannot be "solved" through technical design alone, but must be managed through ongoing institutional processes that determine how objectives are set, how systems are evaluated, and how affected communities can contest or reshape those decisions.
2604.20805.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧（AI）的價值對齊問題通常被框架為一個純粹的技術或規範挑戰，有時專注於假設的未來系統。我主張，這個問題更好地被理解為一個關於治理的結構性問題：不是一個AI系統是否在抽象上對齊，而是它是否對齊得足夠，對誰，及其成本為何。基於經濟學中的委託-代理框架，本文重新概念化了不對齊問題，認為它是在三個相互作用的軸上產生的：目標、信息和委託人。這個三軸框架提供了一種系統化的方法來診斷為什麼在現實世界的系統中會出現不對齊，並澄清了對齊不能被視為模型的單一技術性屬性，而是由目標的具體化、信息的分配以及在實踐中誰的利益被考慮所塑造的結果。本文的核心貢獻在於表明，這種三軸分解意味著對齊根本上是一個治理問題，而不僅僅是工程問題。從這個角度看，對齊本質上是多元的和依賴於上下文的，解決不對齊涉及在競爭價值之間的權衡。由於不對齊可以在每個軸上發生——並且對利益相關者的影響不同——這種結構性描述表明，對齊不能僅通過技術設計來“解決”，而必須通過持續的制度過程來管理，這些過程決定了目標如何設定、系統如何評估，以及受影響社區如何質疑或重塑這些決策。
2604.20805.author: Travis LaCroix et.al.
